mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and higher risk appetite, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days when policy is in an easing trend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative or below unconditional returns in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the Fed's tolerance raises the probability of tighter policy and a higher real discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening consumer demand and labor income, which should support earnings revisions and lift the S&P 500 over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous 3-month gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-upturn cases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, investors should infer weakening income growth and deteriorating cyclical demand, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in triggered unemployment-rising episodes, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weaker current industrial activity and lower cyclically sensitive earnings, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of months when INDPRO is below its 6-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend indicates a housing-led demand slowdown that precedes weaker equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not lower than baseline after HOUST trend breaks, or if the signal loses predictive power in more than two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both weaken together, the economy is losing both output and payroll momentum, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through fast earnings-revision compression.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to produce negative 5-day returns more often than either series alone, or if the short-horizon hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, a stable UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals resilient labor income and lowers recession odds, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays at or below 4.0% while PAYEMS is rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not outperform unconditional 20-day returns, or if positive S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of low-unemployment, rising-payroll instances.
